title: try? silently swallows cipher creation failure in acceptKeyExchange
description: At line 1386, 'self.sslCipher = try? Cipher(...)' uses try? inside a do-catch block. The try? converts any error to nil, so the catch block is never reached. If cipher creation fails, self.sslCipher becomes nil but the code continues, setting encryptionEnabled = true at line 1399. All subsequent encrypt/decrypt calls on nil sslCipher will crash. This is exploitable if an attacker can cause cipher creation to fail (e.g., with crafted key material).
vulnerable_code: self.sslCipher = try? Cipher(cipher: self.cipherType, keyData: derivedKey2, iv: derivedIV2)
recommendation: Change 'try?' to 'try' so cipher creation failures are properly caught and thrown. Add explicit nil check for sslCipher.
